titleStmt.title: "dayof judgment"
Electronic Text Generated from a Transcription of the MS Draft/Fair Copy Name/Number
titleStmt.funder: American Philosophical Society, Beinecke Library, Bibliographical Society of America, Blossman Foundation, Huntington Library, Louisiana Board of Regents, National Endowment for the Humanities, NINES, Pierpont Morgan Library, South Central Modern Language Association, and Southeastern Louisiana University

distributor.name: Southeastern Louisiana University Department of English

msIdentifier.repository: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

msItem.note: For a discussion of foliation, see "Title of Work": Manuscripts.

handNote.p: John Ruskin's early boyhood hand, circa 1826-27, examples of which can be found in MS I, pages ##-##.

lg.l: THE sky was of the hue of blood
The clouds were dark & dun
The slumbering fire on the tempest rode
And crimson was the sun
No night had been so horrible
No breeze had such a sound
For God was on the tempest blast
That parched the panting ground
That billow threw a darkened foam
That was not of the snow
And raised a mournful murmuring moan
As if in deepest woe
Man gazed upon the bloody sky
And on the murky cloud
And felt the burning breeze sweep by
But thought not on his God.
Twas midnight & the tempest blew
The moon looked ghastly pale
Like birds of night the daemons flew
And screamed upon the gale
The earth it shook most dreadfully
And quaked for very fear
The moon became as red as blood
The parting horror is near
The stars fell trembling to the ground
And black became the sun
The earth in inward terror groaned
For lo her hour is come
When sounding oer the mighty sea
And oer the trembling earth
And silencing the thunders roll
The trumpet blast had birth
Then widely yawned the horrid grave
The sea gave up its dead
And from the now departing wave
The righteous raised his head
On that cloud there was a throne
And dazzling was its light
For the Lamb sat there in glory
Than departed suns more bright
Then softly swelled his soothing voice
As he spoke unto the bless'd
Ye righteous & upright rejoice
In everlasting rest
Then rose a heavenly strain
As they sung the works of God
And their unclean sinful souls
All redeemed by his blood
But then he spoke in thunder
Depart accursed to hell
The rocks were rent asunder
And the trembling mountains fell
The accursed in dire confusion driven
Fallen headlong from the skies
The lightnings hurled them from the heaven
To where flames eternal rise
They gnashed their teeth in agony
Blasphemed the holy name
For there the fiery billows rolled
Crowned with a crest of flame
The lake of liquid sulphur broke
In sheets of living fire
And beat against the burning rock
So horrible & dire
Around their heads the thunders break
The living lightnings play
While devils laugh exultingly
And walk the burning way
And mid those flames were hellish forms
The venomed vipers rise
They dart their forked fiery tongues
And flash their flaming eyes
But oh how different the abode
Of those who loved their God
And who have washed their robes of snow
In Christs allsaving blood
Their bosom now no sorrow knows
No scorching tears can spring
For ever freed from all their woes
They touch the sacred string
How loud their golden lyres resound
Oer which their fingers rove
They sing the Saviours agonies
And Gods eternal love
And midst them all the Saviour shone
With grace & love & peace
High on his bright eternal throne
The Sun of Righteousness
They need no sun except their God
Who ever gives them light
No gloom can pierce that bles't abode
And banished is the night
Their state of joy shall know no end
And does the worldly bliss
And they shall sing for evermore
Their Saviour's righteousness
